label: cache proof • hybrid rows need companion AND disk-L2 evidence, gated to the resolved RAM budget
query: Summarize in three sentences how a lock-free queue differs from a mutex-guarded one.
notes: The strong hybrid pin for Qwen 3.5-class rows (Bonsai): companion movement alone is not full reuse proof — older boundaries must demonstrably reach the disk-L2 lane (stores) or come back from it (hits), per the AGENTS.md rule that a KV (or companion) hit alone is not enough for hybrids. The peak-footprint gate is tied to the PRODUCTION-resolved memory-safety load budget — which is the simulated 16 GiB Safe Auto budget when OSAURUS_EVALS_SIM_RAM_GB=16 is in force — so the verdict tracks the same math real loads run under instead of a hand-picked MB. Skipped-with-note on non-hybrid topologies. Off-CI (needs a local model).

followUpTurns: Give one concrete failure mode of each approach.
Which would you pick for a single-producer single-consumer audio thread, and why?
